extra.reasoning: Fever appears only in r4 (priority 100): IF (rash AND vomiting AND dyspnea AND syncope AND hemoptysis AND palpitations AND dizziness AND fever) THEN acuity := self_care. No other rule references fever, so toggling fever can only affect whether r4 fires. The enum order is [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent], low to high, so self_care is the LOWEST acuity. Take the input with rash, vomiting, dyspnea, syncope, hemoptysis, palpitations, dizziness all true and slurred_speech false. With fever=false, r4 does not fire; the firing rules are r1/r2/r5/r7 (urgent, priority 2) and r3/r6/r8 (emergent, priority 3); the highest priority (3) wins, giving acuity=emergent (the maximum). Now set fever=true with everything else unchanged: r4 now fires with priority 100, which beats all priority-3 emergent rules, so acuity := self_care (the minimum). Acuity drops from emergent to self_care purely by setting fever true, directly violating the monotonicity property that setting fever true must never lower acuity. The witness assignment is the fever=true input that yields self_care, whereas its fever=false counterpart yields emergent.
